payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to select work
payload.justification: SG915 establishes claiming as a strategic process performed by children throughout the week.

payload.description: Task definition process for establishing compensated activities
payload.justification: SG916 identifies parental task definition as a strategically motivated process.

payload.description: Reporting process enabling pocket money distribution accuracy
payload.justification: SG919 identifies reporting as a strategic need for enabling accurate distribution.

payload.description: Historical tracking process for past activity visibility
payload.justification: SG920 establishes historical tracking as a strategic requirement, implying a process capability.

payload.description: Pocket money system comprising compensated task units claimable by children and earnings allocated to them
payload.justification: SG915 and SG916 establish tasks as core strategic entities that children claim and parents define with compensation. | SG915 establishes pocket money as the core purpose; SG918 and SG919 reinforce it as a strategic entity requiring transparency and distribution.

payload.description: Task completion status relevant to earnings calculation
payload.justification: SG917 identifies completed work visibility as strategically important, implying completion status as a key attribute.

payload.description: Weekly earnings amount for transparency reporting
payload.justification: SG918 explicitly identifies weekly earnings as requiring transparency, making the amount a strategic attribute.

payload.description: Child claims Task relationship enabling work allocation
payload.justification: SG915 describes children claiming tasks as a core strategic relationship.

payload.description: Weekly cycle governing task claiming and earnings calculation
payload.justification: SG915 mentions tasks throughout the week; SG918 specifies weekly earnings, establishing a weekly strategic cycle.

payload.description: Child actor claiming and completing tasks
payload.justification: SG915, SG917, and SG918 identify children as primary actors claiming tasks and requiring visibility and earnings transparency.

payload.description: Parent actor defining tasks and distributing pocket money
payload.justification: SG916 and SG919 identify parents as actors defining tasks and using reporting for distribution.

payload.description: Enable children to claim tasks for pocket money
payload.justification: SG915 explicitly establishes this as the core strategic purpose.

payload.description: Provide children with visibility into available and completed work, including weekly earnings transparency
payload.justification: SG917 articulates motivational intent for work visibility as a strategic goal. | SG918 explicitly articulates transparency of weekly earnings as a strategic purpose.

payload.description: Enable accurate pocket money distribution through reporting
payload.justification: SG919 identifies the parental need for reporting to enable accurate distribution as a strategic goal.

payload.description: Maintain historical tracking capability for past activities
payload.justification: SG920 expresses historical tracking as a strategic requirement, representing a goal for system capability.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines who participates in the household economy and their respective accountabilities—children as work claimants and performers, parents as work definers and compensation distributors.

payload.name: Work-for-Compensation Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to maintaining a bounded system of compensated work units that children may select, perform, and receive earnings for—establishing the foundational accountability for task availability and completion-based entitlement.

payload.name: Financial Transparency Governance
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility to provide clear visibility into earnings accrual and work history, ensuring all participants can verify compensation accuracy and trace past economic activity within the household.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability for child participation in the household economy through task claiming.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to complete tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability for child participation in the household economy through task completion.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability for parent participation in task definition.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to distribute pocket money.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide capability for parent participation in compensation distribution.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a pocket money system comprising compensated tasks claimable by children and earnings allocated to them.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to maintain the fundamental structural composition of the pocket money system—tasks, children, and earnings—as identified entities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion status for purposes of earnings calculation.
payload.rationale: Enterprise accountability for the structural attribute of tasks that determines earnings entitlement.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish the relationship between children and tasks that enables work allocation.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to the structural relationship enabling children to claim tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to provide the capability for children to select work, satisfying both the process obligation and the motivational goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish compensated tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise accountability for defining the work units that constitute the compensated activities children may perform.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall govern task claiming on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to a temporal governance rule bounding when task claiming occurs.
payload.fit_criteria: Task claiming shall recur at seven-day intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall govern earnings calculation on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to a temporal governance rule bounding when earnings calculation occurs.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings calculation shall recur at seven-day intervals.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable children to view available tasks as a foundation for transparency.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable children to view completed tasks as a foundation for transparency.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into weekly earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to transparency regarding compensation accrual; the What/Attribute CCI identifies the earnings amount as the object of visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable accurate pocket money distribution.
payload.rationale: Enterprise obligation to support distribution accuracy through reporting capability; How/Process CCI identifies the reporting mechanism supporting this goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records of past tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise accountability for preserving task history to support traceability of past economic activity; How/Process CCI identifies the tracking mechanism.
